assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12 shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 41-49 detail each criterion: (1) Single license filter PASS, (2) Comma-separated license filter PASS, (3) Invalid license returns 400 PASS, (4) Pagination integration PASS, (5) Response shape unchanged PASS. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Result: PASS' with detailed evidence for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7 shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 changed files match the task's Files to Modify/Create lists exactly'. Lines 25-26 detail: 'Files modified: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs and modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs. Files created: tests/api/package.rs. No additional files were touched outside the task scope.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5 shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6 shows 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs consist solely of report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all markdown report files. The report discusses reading and analyzing code (e.g., quoting code snippets from list.rs, mod.rs, package.rs) but never mentions modifying, editing, or writing to any code file. All output files are verification/analysis documents.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary paragraph but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no mention of merging. The report presents findings (domain findings, acceptance criteria verdicts, etc.) for human review without taking any merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10 shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, private keys, or credentials found in the diff'. Lines 33-34 elaborate: 'The diff was scanned for passwords, API keys, private keys, tokens, secrets, and credential patterns. None were found. The only string literals are SPDX license identifiers (MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0-only, INVALID-999), test package names, API paths, and error messages.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file tests/api/package.rs with 4 new tests; no existing tests modified or removed'. Lines 67-68 elaborate: 'ADDITIVE -- the PR creates a new test file (tests/api/package.rs) with 4 new integration tests. No existing tests were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all required verdicts: Scope Containment (line 7), Diff Size (line 8), Commit Traceability (line 9), Sensitive Patterns (line 10), CI Status (line 11), Acceptance Criteria (line 12), Test Quality (line 13, noting 'Eval Quality: N/A'), Test Change Classification (line 14), Verification Commands (line 15). Domain Findings section (lines 23-68) organizes these under Intent Alignment (Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), Security (Sensitive Pattern Scan), Correctness (CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), and Style/Conventions (Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Domain Findings section (lines 23-68) provides: Intent Alignment with file-by-file scope comparison listing all 3 files (lines 25-26), diff size with line count breakdown (~80 lines, describing what each portion covers, lines 28-29), and commit traceability with the specific PR URL (line 31). Security with specific pattern types scanned and the actual string literals found in the diff (lines 33-34). Correctness with per-criterion code-level verification including code snippets and test assertions for all 5 criteria (lines 39-51). Style/Conventions with repetitive test detection noting 4 distinct scenarios (lines 57-58), test documentation quoting all 4 doc comments (lines 59-64), and test change classification (lines 67-68). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide even more detailed per-criterion evidence with code excerpts.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 shows 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments and follow Given/When/Then structure; Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 65 states: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist on this PR.' Test Quality is PASS despite Eval Quality being N/A, confirming Eval Quality does not affect the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 65 shows 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist on this PR.' Line 6 shows 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created'. No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. The absence of sub-task creation is consistent with Eval Quality being N/A rather than WARN.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6 show 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the Domain Findings section (which starts at line 23), and are not listed under any of the four domain sub-sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions), indicating they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
